title: Strait Of Hormuz Summit, Fuel Relief For Aussies, Artemis II Launch | 10’s Late News
description: Tonight on 10’s Late News - Australia has joined a virtual meeting of 35 countries, seeking to find a way to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and ease global shocks in oil prices, more relief for drivers is on the way, with motorists set to save nearly 6 cents more a litre on fuel until the end of June, three years after an inquiry called for drastic reform to Australia's gambling industry, the Prime Minister has unveiled the government's long awaited response, NASA's Artemis II launched today, three teens have faced court, accused of committing a string of terrifying break ins across New South Wales and Queensland, at least one person is dead and buildings have been destroyed after a 7.4 magnitude quake struck Indonesia, as Aussies continue to feel the brunt of the conflict in the Middle East, our Prime Minister is now openly questioning the direction and goals of the US, and alleged Bondi terrorist Naveed Akram has failed in his attempt to suppress the identity of his mother, brother and sister for a period of 40 years. title: Trump Slams NATO As “Paper Tiger” Amid Iran Conflict, Mocks Allies | 10 News
description: Donald Trump has intensified his attack on NATO, branding the alliance a “paper tiger” and accusing allies of failing to support US operations in Iran.  Speaking in Washington, he claimed the US had “blasted” Iran and was close to ending the conflict without needing NATO help.  He singled out France’s Emmanuel Macron, lashing out with personal attacks while claiming Paris refused to send support until after the war before mocking the UK over delays in deploying forces.  Trump also criticised past US aid to Ukraine, while praising the American military as the “greatest ever” despite deepening tensions with allies.
